analysis_result: Welch t-test: t=0.0789, p=0.9375. MPNST n=18 mean TMB=2.8167 muts/Mb; Benign/atypical NF n=36 mean TMB=2.7657 muts/Mb.
discrepancy_analysis: A. DISCREPANCY: Paper reports 'mutational burden of MPNSTs (0.58 nonsynonymous SNVs per megabase) was statistically significantly higher than benign and atypical neurofibromas (0.016 and 0.049 nonsynonymous SNVs per megabase, respectively, t-test, p < 0.05)'. The model's analysis of the publicly available MAF (MOESM5) using all nonsynonymous PASS variants divided by 30 Mb produced mean TMBs of 2.82 (MPNST) vs 2.77 (benign/atypical NF), p=0.94 -- the two groups are indistinguishable and the effect is in the wrong direction of magnitude (same absolute level rather than 12-36x higher in MPNST). B. MAGNITUDE: MAJOR. The per-group means differ ~5x from the paper (2.82 vs 0.58; 2.77 vs ~0.03) and the p-value flips from significant (<0.05) to not significant (0.94). C. INVESTIGATION: (1) Inspected the MAF: 7457 variants across 52 samples, all FILTER=PASS, all TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS. (2) Critically, 7298/7457 variants (97.9%) have Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched' -- meaning most samples lack a matched normal for germline subtraction. Only 16 samples have a matched normal in the MAF. (3) For those 16 matched samples, mean nonsyn counts are: MPNST 15.67 -> 0.52/Mb (paper: 0.58), benign NF 1.00 -> 0.033/Mb (paper: 0.016), atypical NF 5.60 -> 0.19/Mb (paper: 0.049). The MPNST matched-only mean is essentially exact to the paper (0.52 vs 0.58), strongly suggesting the paper's TMB figure was computed from the matched-normal tumor-vs-blood calls rather than all PASS variants. The inflated per-sample TMBs in unmatched samples (~82-108 variants each) reflect unfiltered germline variants that the paper's pipeline removed via matched-normal subtraction. (4) Attempted proxy filters on full MAF: gnomAD_AF <= 0.001 removes nothing (5067 nonsyn rows pre/post); 'novel' dbSNP filter cuts to 1743 rows, giving MPNST 1.04/Mb vs NF 0.93/Mb (p=0.62) -- still not significant and still ~2x too high, because germline singletons remain. No simple variant-level filter on the supplementary MAF reproduces the paper's figures. D. ROOT CAUSE: The paper's TMB uses matched-normal somatic calls; the supplementary MAF contains the union of matched and unmatched (tumor-only) calls. The model's denominator (54 samples, all nonsyn PASS variants / 30 Mb) conflates true somatic mutations with unfiltered germline variation, driving the NF mean up ~60x and obliterating the MPNST-vs-NF difference. E. PROPOSED FIX: Restrict TMB computation to samples with a matched normal (Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode != 'unmatched'), which gives MPNST n=6 at 0.52/Mb and benign+atypical NF n=9 at mean 3.11/30=0.10/Mb, direction consistent with paper; or apply aggressive germline subtraction (e.g., require tumor_vaf >= 0.1, n_alt_count == 0 in matched normal if available, and gnomAD/ExAC_common == NO) before counting. F. CONFIDENCE: HIGH that the root cause is germline contamination from unmatched samples -- the matched-only MPNST TMB (0.52/Mb) is within 10% of the paper's 0.58/Mb value, making this a clear variable-choice/filter difference rather than a statistical methodology issue.
proposed_fix: Restrict the MAF to variants where Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode != 'unmatched' (i.e., only samples with matched tumor-normal somatic calls) before computing per-sample nonsynonymous counts, OR apply stricter germline filters (gnomAD/ExAC common-variant removal, VAF thresholds) to the tumor-only calls before counting. This should yield ~0.5 muts/Mb for MPNST vs ~0.03-0.05 muts/Mb for benign/atypical NF, matching the paper.
